reasoning: The 10-year inflation-indexed yield (real yield) as of 2025-10-24 is 1.73%. Recent trends show a rebound from a local low of 1.68% on Oct 22, with a statistical anchor (FRED tool) suggesting a 63% probability of being above 1.73% by Nov 02. Market sentiment, as reflected in recent Reuters and T. Rowe Price reports, highlights fiscal concerns and 'sticky' inflation that may blunt Fed easing and keep long-term real yields elevated. Historical data from 2024 also showed an upward trajectory in real yields during the Nov-Jan period. Probabilities are set to reflect this upward bias while regressing toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon expands and uncertainty regarding the 2025/26 policy transition increases.
